name: Dong Fu (Mao’an)
page_title: Dong Fu (Mao’an) 董扶 (茂安) [Han, Liu Yan] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: Colonel of the State of Shu Commandary
historical_notes:
Dong Fu was a native of Guanghan and pupil of the famed scholar Yang Xu. Both he and another student, Ren An, traveled to Luoyang to learn at the Imperial Academy. They also studied charts and prophesies under Yang Xu.

Dong Fu was eventually appointed as a palace attendant under Emperor Ling. While serving in this capacity, he predicted the fall of the Han Dynasty. At the time, he told Liu Yan that a Son of Heaven would come from Yi Province. Liu Yan hoped to capitalize on this prediction, and moved to Yi. Dong Fu served Liu Yan for a short time, but soon resigned. For the remainder of his life, he taught students in Yi.

Source: The Talent of Shu: Qiao Zhou and the Intellectual World of Early Medieval Sichuan by J. Michael Farmer

sections.short-biography: Famed teacher and debater, he advised Liu Yan to go to Yi.

full_text:
Dong Fu (Mao’an) 董扶 (茂安)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Dong Fu (董扶), styledMao’an (茂安), was born in Guanghan. Famed teacher and debater, he advised Liu Yan to go to Yi. Dong Fu was affiliated with the Han Dynasty and Liu Yan.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Dong Fu was a native of Guanghan and pupil of the famed scholar Yang Xu. Both he and another student, Ren An, traveled to Luoyang to learn at the Imperial Academy. They also studied charts and prophesies under Yang Xu.
Dong Fu was eventually appointed as a palace attendant under Emperor Ling. While serving in this capacity, he predicted the fall of the Han Dynasty. At the time, he told Liu Yan that a Son of Heaven would come from Yi Province. Liu Yan hoped to capitalize on this prediction, and moved to Yi. Dong Fu served Liu Yan for a short time, but soon resigned. For the remainder of his life, he taught students in Yi.
Source:The Talent of Shu: Qiao Zhou and the Intellectual World of Early Medieval Sichuanby J. Michael Farmer
